steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b=f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute the given information
steps.content: The slope is $-48$, so $m=-48$. The line passes through $(0,0)$, so $f(0)=0$ and $b=0$. Therefore $$f(x)=-48x.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: $f(x)=-48x$ is the correct answer. The other choices have slopes $-32$, $-16$, and $1$, so they cannot match the given slope. A is correct.
